title: podman image not known
body:
**Description**

Podman is throwing errors that the image specified in run command is not known. This happens for multiple images. Podman pull works just fine.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
```
root@fedora ~]# podman  run -v openvpn-config:/etc/openvpn -d -p 1194:1194/udp --cap-add=NET_ADMIN docker.io/kylemanna/openvpn:latest
Error: docker.io/kylemanna/openvpn:latest: image not known
[root@fedora ~]# podman  run -v openvpn-config:/etc/openvpn -d -p 1194:1194/udp --cap-add=NET_ADMIN kylemanna/openvpn:latest
Error: kylemanna/openvpn:latest: image not known
[root@fedora ~]# podman  run -v openvpn-config:/etc/openvpn -d -p 1194:1194/udp --cap-add=NET_ADMIN kylemanna/openvpn
Error: kylemanna/openvpn: image not known
[root@fedora ~]# podman pull kylemanna/openvpn
✔ docker.io/kylemanna/openvpn:latest
Trying to pull docker.io/kylemanna/openvpn:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 188c0c94c7c5 skipped: already exists
Copying blob cb26244a2b2a skipped: already exists
Copying blob e470f824352c skipped: already exists
Copying blob 74586f3c5cd4 [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
Copying blob d6ed0c7c142e [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
Copying config 3d5a3627a7 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
3d5a3627a7f6e8954501a4d01342591b09e53710cc5a5e9fc549b6553789ada0

[root@fedora ~]# podman -v
podman version 3.2.1
```


**Describe the results you received:**
Podman throws an error saying the image is not known.

**Describe the results you expected:**
Podman is both aware of how to pull the image during a podman run or can recognize the image is already pulled down locally.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
[root@fedora ~]# podman version
Version:      3.2.1
API Version:  3.2.1
Go Version:   go1.16.3
Built:        Mon Jun 14 19:13:37 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
[root@fedora ~]# rpm -q podman
podman-3.2.1-1.fc34.aarch64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

[root@fedora ~]# grep -v "^#" /etc/containers/registries.conf 
unqualified-search-registries = ["registry.fedoraproject.org", "registry.access.redhat.com", "docker.io", "quay.io" , "ghcr.io"]

short-name-mode="enforcing"

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Fedora IoT

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out!

We had a couple of regressions in image handling, all of which have been fixed in the meantime (including this bug).  Please update to podman v3.2.3.
